title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
7 Tips That Make Working from Home Easy
The pandemic brought too many unprecedented changes in our lives, and some of these changes are here to stay. Because of the worldwide lockdown, businesses introduced the work-from-home model to safeguard their employees from the virus, and that's how work-from-home jobs became the new normal in just about every industry. It's been around 3 years now, and people are still working from home seamlessly, so much so that commutation is now being considered a deterrent, and there are companies that are sticking to the post-covid office arrangement, if not completely then partially.
However, there are a group of people who enjoy working from home, whereas a group misses going to the office, chit chat with friends, having in-person team meetings, and so forth. A large number of workplaces are planning to come up with a hybrid model where employees would come to the office for a few days in the week and would work from home for the remaining days. So, here are a few tips and tricks to make working from home easier.
How to make work from home easy?
Working from home can be difficult. It requires dedication, discipline, and health consciousness. People often feel disconnected, alone, and left out, and their productivity may degrade. Keeping these factors in mind, below are a few tips to make work from home comfortable and effective:
Make your house WFH ready:When you know that working from home is here to stay, investing in a few basics becomes necessary. Invest in good quality tools such as a suitable desk, an ergonomic chair, a mobile stand, adequate stationery items etc., making things simpler and easier.
Designate a peaceful place:
The foremost tip to make work from home easier and more productive is designating a dedicated space as your workstation. Arrange a desk and a comfortable chair with good lighting. You may add some professional decor to give a proper feel to the ‘office’. Working from a bed or a sofa may make you feel lazy and distracted. It also damages your body structure. A dedicated workspace helps you attain focus and work comfortably.
Make a regular schedule:
Sitting to work the minute you open your eyes isn’t the way to go. Maintaining a schedule regularly keeps you motivated and gives your day a proper structure. Get up early, complete your morning routine, get dressed, and then start with your work. During your working hour, keep your phones away to avoid any distractions. Follow your schedule, complete all your tasks on time without procrastinating and log out on time. Don’t forget you have a life after office. That is how you can make work from home easy.
Take well-defined breaks:
You may feel that working non-stop increases your productivity, but your physical and mental health may suffer in the long run. Hence, it is crucial to take small breaks in the middle of the work and stretch your body from time to time; it protects your eyes from continuously staring at the laptop screen and saves your body from sitting in a similar position for the long term. This way, you feel more energetic and work effectively.
Set some basic rules:
When you work from home, it gets difficult to avoid distractions as you stay with your family, and if you have children, it's even more difficult as they keep roaming around, keep talking, and disturbing. That is why you need to discuss it with your family. Inform them about your working hours, and make them aware of the things not to do while you are on calls. Make some rules for yourself too:
Do not take timelines for granted. Eat, work and sleep on time
Keep distractions away, and make it harder to be on social media when working
Plan ahead
Pretend that you are in your office.
Take leaves:
People often feel that taking a day off is unnecessary as they are already at home. Some even work when they are sick. Well. Taking a leave while sick will help you get better, and rest or refreshment will recharge you, increasing productivity. This way, you will perform more effectively.
Do not underestimate socialising:
Socialising is imperative for a good state of mind. Working from home can often leave you alone, isolated, and disconnected. Going out with colleagues or friends or meeting with them informally on an online platform helps you know each other better. You do not feel alone; you feel comfortable working with your subordinates and superiors. You feel someone is there for you, which uplifts your mood and motivates you.
Conclusion
No matter if you are a newbie in the work-from-home environment or are well settled in this new normal now, you need to acknowledge that working from home and maintaining productivity may not also go hand in hand. While the challenges can be aplenty, the given tips will surely help you make your work from home easy, efficacious, and joyful.
Disclaimer:

full_pdf_text:
How To Check Your PAN Card Application Status?
PAN card treated as a valid identity proof for most scenarios in India, It is a 10-digit alphanumeric number that is unique and issued by the Income Tax Department. PAN or Permanent Account Number enables the Income tax department to identify/ link all transactions of the PAN holder. These transactions include tax payments, TDS/TCS credits, returns of income, specified transactions, correspondence etc, and so on.
Though PAN is a number, the PAN Card is a physical card that contains details such as your PAN, name, date of birth, a photograph and the name of your father. The card holds good as proof of identity or for date of birth.
The concept of PAN was introduced by the government of India in 1972 and made statutory under section 139 A of the Income Tax Act w.e.f. 1st April 1976. Section 139A of the Income Tax Act, 1961, lays down various conditions under which you are required to obtain a PAN. You are required to mention your PAN in all communications with the Income Tax Department and while entering into specified financial transactions1. If you do not already have a PAN card, you can apply for one or can apply for a duplicate PAN if you have lost your existing card.
How to Apply For PAN Card?
You can visit the local PAN agencies of your district to apply for PAN offline. You may apply for a new PAN card or update correct information on your existing one or even apply for a duplicate card.
Alternatively, you can apply for PAN online through NSDL or UTIITSL. You may take the help of any of the two agencies mentioned above. You would need to fill out Form 49A which is an application for allotment of PAN and submit relevant documents.
What is PAN Acknowledgement Number?
On successful completion of the application for a PAN card, an applicant receives a 15-digit unique number. This unique code is known as the PAN acknowledgement number. The ones who apply for PAN online through NSDL, can then use the acknowledgement number to track their PAN card status online.
The usage of the acknowledgement number is not limited to just checking pan card status. You can also use the number to download e-PAN card. You may do this within a month of the issuance of a new card or for an updated card as well. And it can be used in both the NSDL and UTIISL websites.
How to Check PAN Application Status?
Whether you have applied for a new PAN card or a duplicate PAN card, you can find out your pan card application status with a few simple steps. You can check the PAN card application status either by calling, through SMS or online.
1. Call
You can contact the call centre number of TIN at +912027218080 and provide your acknowledgement number from your PAN application which is a 15-digit number on the application. 2. SMS
To check the status of your PAN card application via SMS, you can send your 15-digit acknowledgement number to 57575. You will receive back an SMS updating the status of your request. 3. Online
You can also check the status of your Pan card application online via the website of the service provider whose services you may have availed to apply for the PAN card. It can be any of the following2:
● Protean ● UTIITSL Steps to Check PAN Card Status On NDSL Website
Individuals who have applied for their PAN card from NDSL can check the status of their PAN application on the NDSL website. Here are the steps to check the status.
1. Access the NDSL portal https://tin.tin.nsdl.com/pantan/StatusTrack.html 2. Select Application Type as ‘Pan-New / Change Request’ 3. Provide your acknowledgement number 4. Type in the Captcha in the box provided 5. Click on the submit button
Steps to Check PAN Card Status On UTI Website
Taxpayers who have applied for their Pan card using the UTI website need to keep their application coupon number handy. Here are the steps3 to get online status.
1. Access the UTIITSL portal https://www.trackpan.utiitsl.com/PANONLINE/#forward 2. Enter your application coupon number if it is a new application and your PAN number if it is a
duplicate application 3. Enter Date of Birth /Incorporation / Agreement / Partnership or Trust Deed / Formation of Body
of Individuals / Association of Persons, as applicable 4. Type in the Captcha in the box provided 5. Click on the submit button to view the status
Conclusion
Not having a PAN card can make things a little bit tricky for you since it is needed for any financial transactions. Thus, you can consider applying for a PAN card at the earliest. And if you do so, you can check the status of your application online using the above methods.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. pan.aspx#:~:text=Online%20application%20can%20be%20made,%E2%80%8B%E2%80%8B% E2%80%8B%E2%80%8B%E2%80%8B). 3. https://www.trackpan.utiitsl.com/PANONLINE/#forward
